research: PDX (Kuffo, Krippner, Boncz; SIGMOD 2025, arXiv:2503.04422). Dimension-major (vertical) block layout: vectors grouped into blocks; within a block, stored transposed (all vectors' dim 0, then dim 1, ...). Distance over a block = loop over dims with inner loop over vectors (multiple-vectors-at-a-time), which autovectorizes and needs no per-vector horizontal reduction. Training-free, scalar autovectorized (no intrinsics).
note: PDX exact scan: ~2x throughput vs horizontal (18.4 vs 9.3 QPS) and ~3x lower single-query latency (233 vs 715ms), recall identical (1.0). Beats our bandwidth-optimized tiled horizontal too (18.4 vs 14.9). Latency win (3x) > throughput win (2x): single-thread PDX is very efficient (no horizontal reduction), but the 8-core batch re-saturates memory bandwidth (PDX reads the full base too). Block size insensitive (32-256 ~equal). Sets up PDX+pruning (033).
